# Import Requirements Quality Checklist: Work Item Import Support

**Purpose**: Validate completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurability of the work item import requirements before implementation/review gating.
**Created**: 2026-05-17
**Feature**: `specs/035-workitem-import-support/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are explicit requirements defined for all prepare-phase blocking categories (nodes, work item types, missing package artefacts, transform field mismatches)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, §FR-003, §FR-004, §FR-005a-d]
- [ ] CHK002 Are node-readiness strategy requirements complete for both referenced-path creation and full source-tree replication, including selection criteria? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK003 Are checkpoint requirements complete across all replay stages, including stage names and resume semantics? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for link, attachment, and embedded-image replay behavior for both enabled and disabled lever states? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015, §FR-017, §FR-018, §FR-019, §FR-020, §FR-021]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is “clear ready-or-blocked result” defined with explicit report fields or status vocabulary so outcomes are unambiguous? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §SC-001, §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK006 Is “deterministic package order” specified precisely enough to avoid ambiguity about ordering source (folder name, manifest order, or timestamp)? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK007 Is “equivalent observable outcomes” across connectors defined with measurable equivalence criteria and tolerance boundaries? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-026, §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the “skip-or-halt policy” referenced in edge cases defined in normative requirements with explicit decision rules per failure class? [Gap, Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Do requirements consistently state whether unresolved identities are non-blocking in prepare yet still deterministic during import replay? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, §FR-012, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK010 Are FieldTransform failure semantics consistent between prepare-time validation and import-time halt behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005d, §FR-024]
- [ ] CHK011 Do scope boundaries for in-scope and out-of-scope capabilities remain consistent across functional requirements and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-027, §FR-028, §Assumptions]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK012 Are success criteria written so each can be objectively evaluated without implicit interpretation by reviewers? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-§SC-007]
- [ ] CHK013 Do success criteria include measurable pass/fail thresholds for “no manual cleanup” and similar qualitative outcomes? [Gap, Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK014 Is there explicit traceability from each P1 user-story acceptance scenario to at least one functional requirement and one measurable outcome? [Traceability, Gap, Spec §User Scenarios, §FR-001-§FR-028, §SC-001-§SC-007]

## Scenario & Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK015 Are recovery requirements complete for interruption/resume across node readiness, revision replay, and artefact replay stages? [Coverage, Recovery, Spec §US2 Scenario 4, §US3 Scenario 4, §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK016 Are exception-flow requirements complete for missing revision folders, missing binaries, unsupported types, and unresolved translated paths? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-004, §FR-005a-c]
- [ ] CHK017 Are alternate-flow requirements explicit for each extension lever combination so “skip only disabled category” is consistently specified? [Coverage, Alternate Flow, Spec §FR-015-§FR-023, §SC-005]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK018 Are non-functional performance constraints for streaming import and checkpoint latency defined in the requirements set (not only in plan context)? [Gap, Non-Functional, Plan §Technical Context, Spec §FR-001-§FR-028]
- [ ] CHK019 Are observability requirements (logs/metrics/traces/progress events) specified as verifiable feature requirements rather than implied implementation tasks? [Gap, Non-Functional, Plan §Technical Context, Tasks §OpenTelemetry Instrumentation]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK020 Are dependency contracts for identity mapping, NodeTranslation, and FieldTransform documented with required preconditions and failure ownership? [Dependencies, Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-008, §FR-012, §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK021 Are assumptions about export completeness and target-project readiness paired with explicit validation requirements and operator responsibility boundaries? [Assumption, Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-001, §FR-002]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK022 Does the spec resolve whether Team Foundation Server support under this increment is package-import compatibility only or includes connector parity obligations beyond package semantics? [Ambiguity, Conflict, Spec §FR-026, §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK023 Is there any conflict between “must not consult source system” and operational recovery/error handling expectations that might require external lookup? [Conflict, Spec §FR-025, §Edge Cases]

## Reconciliation Notes (2026-05-17)

- `/speckit.analyze` confirmed unresolved contradiction for TFS scope (source-only assumption vs connector parity tasks).
- `/speckit.analyze` also confirmed a critical coverage gap in task decomposition for explicit link-replay requirements traceability.
- Treat CHK022 and CHK014 as active blockers for declaring the spec fully reconciled.
